# iaas_image (Resource)

Captures a custom image (snapshot) from one of your instances. Creation is asynchronous: the image row is created immediately with status "creating" and this resource waits for the hypervisor to finish the capture (status "available") or report a failure (status "error"). There is no update endpoint for images, so every input forces a new resource. Distinct from the `iaas_image` data source, which looks up an existing catalog/user image by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Capture a custom image (snapshot) from an existing instance. Creation is
# asynchronous: the resource waits for the hypervisor to finish the capture
# (status "available") before returning. There is no update endpoint, so any
# change to name/instance_id/cloudinit/type forces a new resource.
resource "iaas_image" "web_snapshot" {
  instance_id = iaas_instance.web.id
  name        = "web-prod-2024-05"

  # cloudinit = true  # defaults to the source instance's cloud-init setting
  # type      = "linux" # linux | windows | other; defaults to the source instance's type
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `instance_id` (String) UUID of an instance you own to snapshot. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) Display name for the resulting image (max 255 characters). There is no rename endpoint, so changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `cloudinit` (Boolean) Whether the resulting image is marked cloud-init enabled. Defaults to the source instance's image setting when omitted. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `type` (String) OS family override: `linux`, `windows`, or `other`. Defaults to the source instance's image type when omitted. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the image, assigned by the API.
- `size` (Number) Captured size of the image in bytes, populated once capture completes. Server-mutable.
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status: "creating", "available", or "error". Server-mutable.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
